title: Macaranga hoffmannii
taxonomy: Macaranga hoffmannii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This family comprises about 3,000 species of plants, many of which are shrubs or trees with milky sap. Macaranga hoffmannii itself is a species of tree native to Southeast Asia. The genus Macaranga is divided into several species, many of which are epiphytes or vines. The exact classification of Macaranga hoffmannii within the broader Euphorbiaceae family is not specified in my current knowledge.
uses: Macaranga hoffmannii, a species of plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.
